zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Alexander Stewart of Bonkyll於1271年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾德雷時，他的父親Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies26歲，母親Lady Margaret de Bonkyl22歲。他於1290年與Jean FitzJames在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾格倫維結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1319年在英國蘇格蘭貝維其可爾享年48歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Alexander Stewart of Bonkyll于1271年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾德雷时，他的父亲Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies26岁，母亲Lady Margaret de Bonkyl22岁。他于1290年与Jean FitzJames在英国苏格兰阿爾迪史爾格倫維结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1319年在英国苏格兰貝維其可爾享年48岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Alexander Stewart of Bonkyll was born in 1271, in Dreghorn, Ayrshire, Scotland, his father, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, was 26 and his mother, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, was 22. He married Jean FitzJames in 1290, in Glenbervie, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1319, in Berwickshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 48, and was buried in Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Alexander Stewart of Bonkyll est né en 1271, à Dreghorn, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Jean FitzJames en 1290, à Glenbervie, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1319, à Berwickshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Alexander Stewart of Bonkyll 1271 in Dreghorn, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies 26 und seine Mutter Lady Margaret de Bonkyl 22. Er hat 1290 in Glenbervie, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Jean FitzJames geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1319 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Berwickshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Alexander Stewart of Bonkyll è nato il nel 1271 nel Dreghorn, Ayrshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Jean FitzJames nel 1290, nel Glenbervie, Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1319, nel Berwickshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolto a Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander Stewart of Bonkyllが1271年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアドレグホールンで生まれた時，父Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garliesは26歳，母Lady Margaret de Bonkylは22歳であった。1290年にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアグレンバーヴィでJean FitzJamesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1319年にイギリススコットランドバーウィクシュアイアで48歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander Stewart of Bonkyll(이)가 1271년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 드레고른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margaret de Bonkyl(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1290년 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 글렌버비에서 Jean FitzJames(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1319년 영국 스코틀랜드 버윅실르에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Alexander Stewart of Bonkyll nasceu em 1271, em Dreghorn, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Jean FitzJames em 1290, em Glenbervie, Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1319, em Berwickshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 48 anos, e foi sepultado em Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Alexander Stewart of Bonkyll родился в 1271 г. в местности дрегаурн, аиршир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Jean FitzJames в 1290 г. в местности гленбирви, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1319 г. в местности бирвиккшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Alexander Stewart of Bonkyll nació en 1271, en Dreghorn, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Jean FitzJames en 1290, en Glenbervie, Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1319, en Berwickshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultado en Escocia, Reino Unido.
